<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1`

```text
1	# 10_tools.ps1 — activate CLI tools. Each block no-ops if the tool is absent,
2	# so a partial install (or the minimal role) never errors the prompt.
3	#
4	# Tool `init` output (starship/zoxide/atuin/direnv/tv) is cached under
5	# $XDG_CACHE_HOME/pwsh-init so a warm shell dot-sources a static file instead of
6	# cold-spawning the binary on every start — the dominant pwsh-startup cost on a
7	# fresh box (each spawn is a process launch + Defender scan). The cache
8	# auto-regenerates when the tool's exe timestamp changes (e.g. a scoop upgrade);
9	# delete ~/.cache/pwsh-init to force a rebuild.
10	function Import-CachedInit {
11	    param(
12	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name,
13	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Exe,
14	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][scriptblock]$Generate
15	    )
16	    $cmd = Get-Command $Exe -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
17	    if (-not $cmd) { return }
18	    $cacheDir  = Join-Path $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME 'pwsh-init'
19	    $cacheFile = Join-Path $cacheDir "$Name.ps1"
20	    $stampFile = "$cacheFile.stamp"
21	    try { $stamp = (Get-Item -LiteralPath $cmd.Source).LastWriteTimeUtc.Ticks.ToString() } catch { $stamp = '' }
22	    $fresh = (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheFile) -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $stampFile) -and
23	             ((Get-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim() -eq $stamp)
24	    if (-not $fresh) {
25	        try {
26	            $out = & $Generate | Out-String
27	            if (-not $out.Trim()) { return }   # tool errored / empty output — retry next start
28	            if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $cacheDir | Out-Null }
29	            Set-Content -LiteralPath $cacheFile -Value $out   -Encoding utf8
30	            Set-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Value $stamp -Encoding utf8
31	        } catch { return }
32	    }
33	    try { . $cacheFile } catch { Write-Warning "profile: cached init '$Name' failed: $_" }
34	}
35	
36	# starship prompt
37	Import-CachedInit -Name 'starship' -Exe 'starship' -Generate { starship init powershell }
38	
39	# zoxide — smarter cd (rebinds `cd` to `z`)
40	Import-CachedInit -Name 'zoxide' -Exe 'zoxide' -Generate { zoxide init powershell --cmd cd }
41	
42	# Runtimes (node/bun/go/rust/ruby) are native via scoop and Python via uv — no
43	# mise on Windows (its activate/shims are unreliable here). See docs/rationale.
44	
45	# atuin — SQLite shell history with fuzzy search (Ctrl+R)
46	Import-CachedInit -Name 'atuin' -Exe 'atuin' -Generate { atuin init powershell }
47	
48	# fzf — fuzzy finder + PSFzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history)
49	if (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
50	    $env:FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS = '--height 40% --layout=reverse --border'
51	    if (Get-Command fd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
52	        $env:FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND = 'fd --hidden --strip-cwd-prefix --exclude .git'
53	    }
54	    # Import directly — a `Get-Module -ListAvailable` guard scans all of
55	    # PSModulePath (slow, esp. under OneDrive-hydrated Documents\PowerShell\Modules).
56	    # `Get-Module -Name` checks only loaded modules, so it's cheap.
57	    Import-Module PSFzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
58	    if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
59	        Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
60	    }
61	}
62	
63	# direnv — per-directory environments
64	Import-CachedInit -Name 'direnv' -Exe 'direnv' -Generate { direnv hook pwsh }
65	
66	# television (tv) — fuzzy picker shell integration. tv 0.15+ renamed the shell
67	# value `powershell` -> `power-shell`; older builds used `powershell`. Try the
68	# new spelling first, fall back, and only cache non-empty output so a version
69	# mismatch never errors the prompt.
70	Import-CachedInit -Name 'tv' -Exe 'tv' -Generate {
71	    $o = tv init power-shell 2>$null | Out-String
72	    if (-not $o.Trim()) { $o = tv init powershell 2>$null | Out-String }
73	    $o
74	}
75
```
</details></tool-use>

1	-- starship prompt for cmd.exe, loaded by Clink from its profile dir
2	-- (%LocalAppData%\clink). Reuses ~/.config/starship.toml -- the SAME config that
3	-- drives pwsh and the macOS/Linux dotfiles -- plus the XDG env already persisted
4	-- to the User registry by run_onchange_after_03_xdg_env.ps1, so the cmd prompt
5	-- matches pwsh with zero extra config.
6	--
7	-- Deployed only when the opt-in `installClink` toggle is on (see .chezmoiignore).
8	-- The zoxide.lua / fzf.lua Clink bridges are fetched from upstream at apply time
9	-- by run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl (they have no package manifest).
10	-- Ref: https://starship.rs/guide/ -> Command Prompt (needs Clink v1.2.30+).
11	load(io.popen('starship init cmd'):read("*a"))()
12

1	# 90_psreadline.ps1 — history, inline predictions, and edit mode.
2	# Templated: edit mode follows the `enableVimMode` init toggle.
3	# PSReadLine ships with pwsh; import directly rather than scanning PSModulePath
4	# with Get-Module -ListAvailable. Get-Module -Name checks only loaded modules.
5	Import-Module PSReadLine -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
6	if (Get-Module -Name PSReadLine) {
7	
8	    Set-PSReadLineOption -PredictionSource HistoryAndPlugin -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
9	    Set-PSReadLineOption -PredictionViewStyle ListView       -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
10	    Set-PSReadLineOption -HistorySearchCursorMovesToEnd
11	    Set-PSReadLineOption -HistoryNoDuplicates
12	
13	    # Up/Down search history by the prefix already typed.
14	    Set-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Key UpArrow   -Function HistorySearchBackward
15	    Set-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Key DownArrow -Function HistorySearchForward
16	{{- if .enableVimMode }}
17	
18	    # vi modal editing (mirrors the shells' enableVimMode on macOS/Linux).
19	    Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Vi
20	    Set-PSReadLineOption -ViModeIndicator Cursor
21	{{- else }}
22	
23	    Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Windows
24	{{- end }}
25	}
26
```

154	
155	# ===========================================================================
156	Ensure-Scoop
157	
158	# Core CLI toolset — the shell baseline, always installed.
159	Scoop-Install @(
160	    'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', 'make', 'just',
161	    'starship', 'zoxide', 'fzf', 'fd', 'ripgrep', 'bat', 'eza', 'jq',
162	    'delta', 'lazygit', 'neovim', 'yazi', 'btop', 'television', 'tlrc',
163	    'uv', 'gnupg',
164	    # Runtimes are NATIVE via scoop, not mise: mise's activate/shims are
165	    # unreliable on Windows and it has no windows-arm64 bun build. node + bun
166	    # here; Python via uv (below); go/rust/ruby under Extra runtimes.
167	    # copilot-proxy needs bun/bunx.
168	    'nodejs-lts', 'bun',
169	    # LazyVim needs a C compiler for tree-sitter parsers (zig), the tree-sitter
170	    # CLI the nvim-treesitter `main` branch requires, and a clipboard provider so
171	    # `unnamedplus` yanks reach the Windows clipboard (win32yank).
172	    'zig', 'tree-sitter', 'win32yank'
173	)
174	
175	# On Windows-on-ARM, scoop's neovim is amd64 (runs under x64 emulation) while
176	# zig installs arm64-native, so zig-built tree-sitter parsers are the wrong
177	# arch for nvim. Add an amd64 gcc that matches the emulated nvim.
178	if ([System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeInformation]::OSArchitecture -eq 'Arm64') {
179	    Info 'ARM64 host: adding amd64 gcc for nvim-treesitter'
180	    Scoop-Install @('gcc')
181	}
182	
183	# gh-dash: GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (`gh dash`). It's a gh extension, not a
184	# scoop package, so it needs gh to be authenticated -- this no-ops on a fresh
185	# machine until `gh auth login`. run_onchange only re-fires when this script
186	# changes, so after authenticating, install by hand (`gh extension install
187	# dlvhdr/gh-dash`) or force a reapply (`chezmoi state delete-bucket
188	# --bucket=entryState`). gh-notify / gh-select from the Unix repo are bash-only
189	# extensions and are intentionally skipped on Windows.
190	if (Have gh) {
191	    gh auth status *> $null
192	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
193	        $ghExts = gh extension list 2>$null | Out-String
194	        if ($ghExts -notmatch 'dlvhdr/gh-dash') {
195	            Info 'gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash'
196	            gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash
197	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'gh-ext:gh-dash' }
198	        }
199	    } else {
200	        Info 'gh not authenticated -- skipping gh-dash (gh auth login, then reapply)'
201	    }
202	}
203	
204	# node/bun come from scoop above (on ~/scoop/shims, reliably on PATH). We do NOT
205	# use mise here -- see the runtimes note in the core scoop list.
206	
207	# Default Python, uv-managed. --default also creates unversioned python/python3
208	# (not just python3.x) in UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR (~/.local/bin, on PATH via 00_env
209	# ahead of the Store's python.exe app-alias). On uv 0.8+ `--default` lives behind
210	# preview mode, so it must be paired with `--preview` (older uv just ignored it) —
211	# without it, current uv errors: "`--default` is only available in preview mode".
212	if (Have uv) {
213	    Info 'uv python install --default --preview'
214	    $env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
215	    uv python install --default --preview 2>&1 | Out-Host
216	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:python' }
217	}
218	
219	# Make PSGallery usable non-interactively before Install-Module (NuGet provider
220	# + trust) so the fzf key bindings / audio cmdlets install reliably.
221	try {
222	    if (-not (Get-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -ListAvailable -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
223	        Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Scope CurrentUser -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
224	    }
225	    if ((Get-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).InstallationPolicy -ne 'Trusted') {
226	        Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
227	    }
228	} catch { Write-Warning "PSGallery prep failed (non-fatal): $_" }
229	
230	# PSReadLine ships with pwsh; PSFzf powers the fzf key bindings in profile.d
231	# (Ctrl+t = files, Ctrl+r = fuzzy history search).
232	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf)) {
233	    Info 'Install-Module PSFzf'
234	    Install-Module PSFzf -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
235	}
236	# AudioDeviceCmdlets: absolute volume + explicit mute for the sys* helpers.
237	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name AudioDeviceCmdlets)) {
238	    Info 'Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets'
239	    Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
240	}
241	
242	{{ if .installExtraRuntimes -}}
243	# Extra language runtimes, native via scoop (rust via rustup). node/bun/uv are
244	# baseline; go/rust/ruby added here.
245	Scoop-Install @('go', 'rustup', 'ruby')
246	{{ end -}}
247	
248	{{ if .installMediaTools -}}
249	Scoop-Install @('ffmpeg', 'imagemagick')
```

361	# Clink = Bash-style line editing for cmd.exe, so starship / zoxide / fzf work in

365	# bridges have no scoop/winget manifest (and zoxide has no native cmd target), so

374	    $clinkPlugins = @{

376	        'fzf.lua'    = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chrisant996/clink-fzf/master/fzf.lua'
377	    }
378	    foreach ($name in $clinkPlugins.Keys) {
379	        Info "clink plugin: $name"
380	        try { Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Uri $clinkPlugins[$name] -OutFile (Join-Path $clinkDir $name) }
381	        catch { Register-Failure "clink-plugin:$name" }
382	    }
383	} else { Register-Failure 'clink (autorun/plugins not configured)' }
384	{{ end -}}
385	
386	if ($script:Failures.Count -gt 0) {
387	    Write-Warning ("package install: {0} item(s) failed and were skipped:" -f $script:Failures.Count)
388	    $script:Failures | ForEach-Object { Write-Warning "  - $_" }
389	    Write-Warning "Re-run 'just apply' (or 'chezmoi apply') to retry, or install them manually."
390	} else {
391	    Info 'package install complete — no failures'
392	}
393
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 05:50:26Z)**_

Let me read the main profile loader to confirm the fragment ordering (sort order determines whether 90_psreadline runs after 10_tools).

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 05:50:26Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`

```text
1	# Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1 — managed by chezmoi (Windows dotfiles).
2	#
3	# Thin loader: dot-sources every fragment in ~/.config/powershell/profile.d in
4	# sorted order, then the user's untracked local override. Keep logic in the
5	# fragments, not here — this file is intentionally boring.
6	
7	$ConfigRoot  = Join-Path $HOME '.config/powershell'
8	$FragmentDir = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'profile.d'
9	$ModuleDir   = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'modules'
10	
11	# Make chezmoi-managed modules importable (e.g. Copilot).
12	if (Test-Path $ModuleDir) {
13	    if (($env:PSModulePath -split [System.IO.Path]::PathSeparator) -notcontains $ModuleDir) {
14	        $env:PSModulePath = $ModuleDir + [System.IO.Path]::PathSeparator + $env:PSModulePath
15	    }
16	}
17	
18	if (Test-Path $FragmentDir) {
19	    foreach ($fragment in Get-ChildItem -Path $FragmentDir -Filter '*.ps1' | Sort-Object Name) {
20	        try { . $fragment.FullName }
21	        catch { Write-Warning "profile.d: failed to load $($fragment.Name): $_" }
22	    }
23	}
24	
25	# Untracked, user-local overrides (secrets, machine-specific tweaks).
26	$LocalProfile = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'local.ps1'
27	if (Test-Path $LocalProfile) { . $LocalProfile }
28
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/shell.md`

```text
147	## cmd.exe via Clink
148	
149	pwsh is the default and primary shell here — but `cmd.exe` still shows up (some
150	tools spawn it, and muscle memory dies hard). The **opt-in `installClink` toggle**
151	gives the DOS prompt a starship prompt plus `z` dir-jumping and Ctrl-R/Ctrl-T
152	fzf. It reuses what pwsh already has: the **same `~/.config/starship.toml`**, and
153	the `XDG_*` vars that `run_onchange_after_03_xdg_env.ps1` persists to the **User
154	registry** — so cmd inherits them with no profile of its own.
155	
156	cmd's line editor is **[Clink](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/)** — its
157	PSReadLine analog. With `installClink` on, the packages script installs Clink
158	(scoop `main`), registers a per-user cmd **AutoRun** (`clink autorun install`, no
159	admin), and populates the Clink profile dir (`%LocalAppData%\clink`):
160	
161	| File | Source | Gives you |
162	|---|---|---|
163	| `starship.lua` | managed by chezmoi (ours) | starship prompt (`starship init cmd`) |
164	| `zoxide.lua` | fetched from `clink-zoxide` at apply | `z` / `zi` directory jumping |
165	| `fzf.lua` | fetched from `clink-fzf` at apply | Ctrl-R history · Ctrl-T files · Alt-C dirs |
166	
167	`starship.lua` is the only piece committed to this repo (a one-line loader that
168	runs `starship init cmd`). The two community bridges have no scoop/winget
169	manifest — and zoxide has no native cmd target — so, like herdr, they're fetched
170	from upstream into `%LocalAppData%\clink` at apply time (network failures are
171	non-fatal; starship still works offline).
172	
173	!!! note "Prompt parity, not feature parity"
174	    cmd gets the **prompt + navigation**, not the pwsh feature set. atuin, direnv,
175	    and Television have no cmd/Clink path, and every PowerShell function/module —
176	    the `ll`/`gs`/`reload` aliases, `y` (yazi), `sysvol`, the `copilot-proxy`
177	    module — is pwsh-only. For the full experience use pwsh; Clink just makes an
178	    unavoidable cmd session pleasant. Inspect it with `clink info` (lists the
179	    profile dir + loaded scripts).
180
```
</details></tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm checking the upstream clink-fzf implementation to understand how Ctrl-R binding works and whether `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` is the right approach. The key requirement is that the fzf binary needs to be available on PATH, which is already installed via scoop.

The critical detail is that `fzf.default_bindings` is a setting registered by fzf.lua itself through `settings.add()`, so the setting only becomes available after fzf.lua has been loaded at least once in a clink session. Running `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` before that initial load won't work because clink doesn't yet know the setting exists.

The installer has an ordering issue: it tries to set `fzf.default_bindings true` before the foreach loop that actually downloads fzf.lua into the profile directory, so the setting registration hasn't happened yet and the command fails with an "unknown setting" error. The setting defaults to false if it was never persisted, which means fzf.lua loads at runtime without the custom bindings enabled, leaving Ctrl-R stuck on readline's default behavior. So the root cause is definitely the ordering issue — trying to set an unknown setting before the script that registers it even exists. So there are two likely causes: either the fzf.lua download failed due to a network issue, which would prevent Ctrl-R from working, or there's an ordering problem where `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` runs before fzf.lua exists, so the setting doesn't get registered and fzf.lua loads with the default false value. The second scenario fits better with the symptom of Ctrl-R not working while other features like starship do work.

I need to check the clink-fzf documentation to understand exactly how the default_bindings mechanism works and confirm this theory.
</details></think>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 05:51:02Z)**_

I have the full picture from the repo. Let me verify the upstream clink-fzf binding mechanism to nail down the root cause.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 05:51:14Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="WebFetch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **WebFetch**</summary>
 `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chrisant996/clink-fzf/master/fzf.lua`

```text
- Which keys are bound and how:
  - The script sets these default bindings (when enabled): Ctrl+T → fzf_file, Ctrl+R → fzf_history, Alt+C → fzf_directory.  
    (See apply_default_bindings where rl.setbinding is called for those keys.)

- Does it require fzf.default_bindings true to auto-bind?
  - Yes. The script only applies those bindings when the setting is enabled. Quote:
    "if settings.get('fzf.default_bindings') then"

- What is the default value of fzf.default_bindings?
  - Default is false. Quote from the settings registration:
    "maybe_add(rl.setbinding and 'fzf.default_bindings', false,"

- Is the setting registered via settings.add inside this script (so clink must load fzf.lua before 'clink set ...' recognizes it)?
  - Yes. The script registers the setting at load time using maybe_add (which calls settings.add when unset). Quote:
    "local function maybe_add(name, ...)" and the registration call:
    "maybe_add(rl.setbinding and 'fzf.default_bindings', false,"

Relevant short quotes (<=125 chars each):
- "if settings.get('fzf.default_bindings') then"
- "maybe_add(rl.setbinding and 'fzf.default_bindings', false,"
- "local function maybe_add(name, ...)"

Summary in plain words:
- Ctrl+T, Ctrl+R, Alt+C are among the default key bindings the script will apply.
- They are only auto-bound if the setting fzf.default_bindings is true.
- That setting defaults to false.
- The script registers the setting at load time (so Clink must load fzf.lua for 'clink set fzf.default_bindings ...' to know about it).
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 05:51:04Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/rationale.md`

```text
1	# Rationale
2	
3	Why this setup makes the choices it does.
4	
5	## PowerShell 7 default, cmd.exe optional (via Clink)
6	
7	`cmd.exe` (the DOS-lineage shell) has no real scripting story: no functions worth
8	the name, no structured data, awkward quoting. **PowerShell 7 (`pwsh`)** is the
9	modern default on Windows — an object pipeline, real modules, cross-platform, and
10	first-class `init` integration for every tool here (starship, zoxide, atuin,
11	fzf, direnv). Windows PowerShell 5.1 still ships in-box but is frozen; we install
12	and target pwsh 7. Everything in this repo — the `$PROFILE` fragments, aliases,
13	functions, copilot-proxy — is written for pwsh.
14	
15	cmd.exe isn't scripted *against*, but it isn't ignored either: some tools spawn it
16	and muscle memory persists. The **opt-in `installClink` toggle** gives the DOS
17	prompt a starship prompt plus `z` dir-jumping and Ctrl-R/Ctrl-T fzf, via
18	[Clink](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/) (cmd's PSReadLine analog). It reuses
19	the **same `starship.toml`** and the XDG env already persisted to the User
20	registry — so the *config* is shared; only the shell-activation "script" differs
21	(Clink Lua vs the pwsh profile). This is **prompt + navigation parity, not feature
22	parity**: atuin / direnv / tv and every pwsh function/module stay pwsh-only. pwsh
23	remains the default and primary target; cmd is a scoped secondary. See
24	[Shell → cmd.exe via Clink](shell.md#cmdexe-via-clink).
25	
26	(WSL is great, but it's a Linux environment — handled by the cross-platform
27	dotfiles, not this repo. This repo is about a good *native* Windows shell.)
28	
29	## scoop for CLIs + winget for apps, not Chocolatey
30
```
</details></tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see the issue now—the `fzf.lua` file registers the `fzf.default_bindings` setting at startup with a default value of false, and only activates the Ctrl-R/Ctrl-T/Alt-C bindings when that setting is explicitly set to true. This means the setting needs to be persisted as true in the configuration, and clink needs to have the fzf.lua module loaded to recognize it in the first place.

The installer has a critical ordering bug: it runs `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` on line 371, but the foreach loop that actually downloads fzf.lua into the profile directory doesn't execute until lines 378-382. When the `clink set` command runs, fzf.lua doesn't exist yet, so clink doesn't recognize the setting and the command fails silently (output redirected to null). The setting never gets written to clink_settings, and when fzf.lua eventually loads at runtime, it defaults to false, leaving Ctrl-R at the readline default behavior. This explains why starship works (deployed by chezmoi before clink runs) but Ctrl-R doesn't—the setting was never actually persisted because it was unknown at the time of the `clink set` call.

The fix is straightforward: move the `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` line to after the download loop completes, so fzf.lua is already in place when clink tries to register the setting. The fzf binary itself is already on PATH from the scoop installation, and there's no existing clink_settings file in the repo that would interfere.

I have all the details needed to write the final report now — exact line numbers, root cause, and answers to all five questions.

## Summary of the Ctrl+R binding chain

### How profile fragments load (ordering is the crux)
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1:19` dot-sources fragments **in sorted-by-name order**:
```
foreach ($fragment in Get-ChildItem -Path $FragmentDir -Filter '*.ps1' | Sort-Object Name) {
```
So the numeric prefixes are the load order. The relevant two run as: **`10_tools.ps1` first, then `90_psreadline.ps1` last.** (`.tmpl` is stripped at render time, so `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` deploys as `90_psreadline.ps1` and matches the `*.ps1` filter.)

### 1. Where PSFzf is activated
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1:48-61`:
```
48  # fzf — fuzzy finder + PSFzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history)
49  if (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
50      $env:FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS = '--height 40% --layout=reverse --border'
51      if (Get-Command fd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
52          $env:FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND = 'fd --hidden --strip-cwd-prefix --exclude .git'
53      }
57      Import-Module PSFzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
58      if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
59          Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
60      }
61  }
```
This is the only place that binds Ctrl+r/Ctrl+t to PSFzf. It runs at prefix **10**.

### 3. The ordering conflict (likely root cause)
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/dot_config/powershell/profile.d/90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` runs *after* 10_tools and calls, at lines 19/23:
```
14      Set-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Key UpArrow   -Function HistorySearchBackward
15      Set-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Key DownArrow -Function HistorySearchForward
16  {{- if .enableVimMode }}
19      Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Vi
...
23      Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Windows
```
`Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode <mode>` **resets PSReadLine's key-handler dispatch tables to that mode's defaults**, which reverts Ctrl+r to the built-in `ReverseSearchHistory` — exactly the `bck-i-search:` prompt the user sees. Because PSFzf's chord is set at fragment **10** and the `-EditMode` call happens at fragment **90 (the last fragment to touch PSReadLine/edit mode)**, the PSFzf Ctrl+r binding is clobbered on every shell start. `98_tv_cache.ps1` is later but does not touch key bindings (verified by grep).

This is the canonical PSFzf gotcha: `Set-PsFzfOption` must run **after** `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode`, but here it runs before. Note this ordering also exposes the Up/Down handlers on lines 14-15, which are likewise set before the EditMode line within the same fragment — supporting the "EditMode reset wins" reading. The fix direction (not applied) would be to set the PSFzf chord after EditMode, e.g. move the PSFzf activation to a fragment numbered above 90, or move the EditMode setting before fragment 10.

### 2. How PSFzf is installed (not gated)
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:230-235`:
```
230  # PSReadLine ships with pwsh; PSFzf powers the fzf key bindings in profile.d
231  # (Ctrl+t = files, Ctrl+r = fuzzy history search).
232  if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf)) {
233      Info 'Install-Module PSFzf'
234      Install-Module PSFzf -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
235  }
```
This is **unconditional** — not behind any `{{ if .someToggle }}` init toggle. It is preceded by PSGallery/NuGet trust prep at lines 219-228 so the install works non-interactively.

### 4. fzf binary via scoop
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:159-163` — `fzf` is in the **core, always-installed** scoop list (not gated):
```
159  Scoop-Install @(
160      'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', 'make', 'just',
161      'starship', 'zoxide', 'fzf', 'fd', 'ripgrep', 'bat', 'eza', 'jq',
```

### 5. Environment / PATH requirements
PSFzf requires the `fzf` binary on PATH; `10_tools.ps1:49` guards the whole block behind `if (Get-Command fzf ...)`, and sets `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS` (line 50) and `FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND` (line 52, when `fd` is present). fzf reaches PATH via scoop shims. No PSFzf-specific env var beyond fzf's presence is required.

### Bottom line
The exact code governing Ctrl+r is `10_tools.ps1:59` (`Set-PsFzfOption ... -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'`). The most likely reason it doesn't take effect is a **fragment-ordering conflict**: `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` loads afterward and its `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Windows/Vi` (lines 19/23) resets PSReadLine key handlers back to defaults, restoring the built-in `bck-i-search:` reverse search and overriding PSFzf. Installation of both `PSFzf` (module) and `fzf` (binary) is present and ungated, so this is a runtime binding-order problem, not a missing-dependency problem.

Note: I did not create a plan file — this task is a read-only investigation and the findings above are the deliverable. No files were modified.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1`

```text
1	# 10_tools.ps1 — activate CLI tools. Each block no-ops if the tool is absent,
2	# so a partial install (or the minimal role) never errors the prompt.
3	#
4	# Tool `init` output (starship/zoxide/atuin/direnv/tv) is cached under
5	# $XDG_CACHE_HOME/pwsh-init so a warm shell dot-sources a static file instead of
6	# cold-spawning the binary on every start — the dominant pwsh-startup cost on a
7	# fresh box (each spawn is a process launch + Defender scan). The cache
8	# auto-regenerates when the tool's exe timestamp changes (e.g. a scoop upgrade);
9	# delete ~/.cache/pwsh-init to force a rebuild.
10	function Import-CachedInit {
11	    param(
12	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name,
13	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Exe,
14	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][scriptblock]$Generate
15	    )
16	    $cmd = Get-Command $Exe -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
17	    if (-not $cmd) { return }
18	    $cacheDir  = Join-Path $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME 'pwsh-init'
19	    $cacheFile = Join-Path $cacheDir "$Name.ps1"
20	    $stampFile = "$cacheFile.stamp"
21	    try { $stamp = (Get-Item -LiteralPath $cmd.Source).LastWriteTimeUtc.Ticks.ToString() } catch { $stamp = '' }
22	    $fresh = (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheFile) -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $stampFile) -and
23	             ((Get-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim() -eq $stamp)
24	    if (-not $fresh) {
25	        try {
26	            $out = & $Generate | Out-String
27	            if (-not $out.Trim()) { return }   # tool errored / empty output — retry next start
28	            if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $cacheDir | Out-Null }
29	            Set-Content -LiteralPath $cacheFile -Value $out   -Encoding utf8
30	            Set-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Value $stamp -Encoding utf8
31	        } catch { return }
32	    }
33	    try { . $cacheFile } catch { Write-Warning "profile: cached init '$Name' failed: $_" }
34	}
35	
36	# starship prompt
37	Import-CachedInit -Name 'starship' -Exe 'starship' -Generate { starship init powershell }
38	
39	# zoxide — smarter cd (rebinds `cd` to `z`)
40	Import-CachedInit -Name 'zoxide' -Exe 'zoxide' -Generate { zoxide init powershell --cmd cd }
41	
42	# Runtimes (node/bun/go/rust/ruby) are native via scoop and Python via uv — no
43	# mise on Windows (its activate/shims are unreliable here). See docs/rationale.
44	
45	# atuin — SQLite shell history with fuzzy search (Ctrl+R)
46	Import-CachedInit -Name 'atuin' -Exe 'atuin' -Generate { atuin init powershell }
47	
48	# fzf — fuzzy finder + PSFzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history)
49	if (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
50	    $env:FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS = '--height 40% --layout=reverse --border'
51	    if (Get-Command fd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
52	        $env:FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND = 'fd --hidden --strip-cwd-prefix --exclude .git'
53	    }
54	    # Import directly — a `Get-Module -ListAvailable` guard scans all of
55	    # PSModulePath (slow, esp. under OneDrive-hydrated Documents\PowerShell\Modules).
56	    # `Get-Module -Name` checks only loaded modules, so it's cheap.
57	    Import-Module PSFzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
58	    if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
59	        Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
60	    }
61	}
62	
63	# direnv — per-directory environments
64	Import-CachedInit -Name 'direnv' -Exe 'direnv' -Generate { direnv hook pwsh }
65	
66	# television (tv) — fuzzy picker shell integration. tv 0.15+ renamed the shell
67	# value `powershell` -> `power-shell`; older builds used `powershell`. Try the
68	# new spelling first, fall back, and only cache non-empty output so a version
69	# mismatch never errors the prompt.
70	Import-CachedInit -Name 'tv' -Exe 'tv' -Generate {
71	    $o = tv init power-shell 2>$null | Out-String
72	    if (-not $o.Trim()) { $o = tv init powershell 2>$null | Out-String }
73	    $o
74	}
75
```
</details></tool-use>

## Clink → fzf wiring in the Windows dotfiles repo

### 1. The Clink setup in the packages installer
`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`, gated block lines 360–384:

```
360  {{ if .installClink -}}
368  Scoop-Install @('clink')
369  if (Have clink) {
370      clink autorun install *> $null                 # HKCU AutoRun; inject into every cmd.exe (no admin)
371      clink set fzf.default_bindings true *> $null    # Ctrl-R history / Ctrl-T files / Alt-C dir
372      $clinkDir = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'clink'
373      New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $clinkDir | Out-Null
374      $clinkPlugins = @{
375          'zoxide.lua' = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shunsambongi/clink-zoxide/master/zoxide.lua'
376          'fzf.lua'    = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chrisant996/clink-fzf/master/fzf.lua'
377      }
378      foreach ($name in $clinkPlugins.Keys) {

- There is no `clink inject` call — injection is via `clink autorun install` (line 370), which registers the HKCU cmd AutoRun so every cmd.exe injects Clink.

- `fzf.lua` ← `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chrisant996/clink-fzf/master/fzf.lua`

## Most likely reason Ctrl+R isn't bound to fzf

**An ordering bug in the installer.** At line 371, `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` runs **before** the `foreach` loop (lines 378–382) that downloads `fzf.lua` into `%LOCALAPPDATA%\clink`. Because `fzf.default_bindings` is a setting *defined inside `fzf.lua`* (via `settings.add`/`maybe_add`), Clink does not recognize that setting name when `fzf.lua` is not yet present in the profile dir. The `clink set …` command therefore errors out ("setting not found") and — with the error swallowed by `*> $null` — the value is never written to the Clink settings file.

At cmd.exe runtime later, `fzf.lua` loads fine (so it's present after the download), registers `fzf.default_bindings` with its **default of `false`**, and its `if settings.get('fzf.default_bindings')` guard is false — so it never calls `rl.setbinding` for Ctrl-R/Ctrl-T/Alt-C. Ctrl-R falls back to readline's built-in `bck-i-search`, exactly as reported.

This is fully consistent with the symptom pair: starship works (chezmoi-deployed `starship.lua` loads via a working autorun), but Ctrl-R doesn't (the `fzf.default_bindings` toggle was never actually persisted).

The fix (for the parent agent, not applied here) is to move `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` to **after** the download loop so `fzf.lua` exists when the setting is written. Secondary things worth checking on the affected machine: whether the `fzf.lua`/`zoxide.lua` downloads succeeded at all (`Register-Failure` would have logged `clink-plugin:fzf.lua`), and `clink info` to confirm `fzf.lua` is in the loaded-scripts list and inspect the persisted `fzf.default_bindings` value.

  "maybe_add(rl.setbinding and 'fzf.default_bindings', false,"

- Key bindings are applied only when that setting is enabled:
  "if settings.get('fzf.default_bindings') then"

Therefore the Ctrl+T / Ctrl+R / Alt+C (and other) bindings are only installed when fzf.default_bindings is true.
```
</details></tool-use>

 `clink set unknown setting name not defined by loaded lua script persist behavior`

```text
Web search results for query: "clink set unknown setting name not defined by loaded lua script persist behavior"

Links: [{"title":"Clink v1.9.28","url":"https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html"},{"title":"Clink v1.9.28","url":"https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html?utm_source=openai"}]

I ran the query and pulled the most relevant results. Top findings (with source links):

- Clink docs — settings API (settings.add): "Adds a setting to the list of Clink settings and includes it in `clink set`." (explains how Lua scripts can register settings so `clink set` shows them). ([chrisant996.github.io](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html))

- Clink docs — settings.set and clink set command: describes the settings API (settings.set) and the `clink set` CLI (list/query/set settings); notes a historical bugfix where settings.set wasn't updating the settings file. ([chrisant996.github.io](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html))

- Clink docs — Lua scripts are executed when loaded and (important) "scripts are also loaded every time `clink set` is run." That affects behavior when settings are defined by Lua scripts. ([chrisant996.github.io](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html))

- Clink docs — settings.get: "Returns the current value of the name Clink setting or nil if the setting does not exist." (so Clink distinguishes between defined vs non-existent settings). ([chrisant996.github.io](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html?utm_source=openai))

What these results imply (summary of the sources above):
- Lua scripts can call settings.add(...) to register settings so they appear in `clink set`. ([chrisant996.github.io](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html))
- settings.set (and the `clink set` command) persist settings to the clink settings store (clink_settings); there have been fixes historically to ensure settings are saved. ([chrisant996.github.io](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html))
- Because Clink reloads scripts when `clink set` runs, settings that are only defined by loaded Lua scripts may be re-registered/affected when scripts are reloaded. ([chrisant996.github.io](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html))
- If a setting name truly does not exist (not registered), settings.get will return nil. ([chrisant996.github.io](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html?utm_source=openai))

```md
# Fix Ctrl+R fzf bindings in both pwsh (PSFzf) and cmd.exe (Clink)

## Context

After the latest bootstrap the user reports Ctrl+R still shows the built-in
`bck-i-search:` reverse search instead of an fzf pane — in **both** PowerShell 7
and cmd.exe. Starship works in cmd, so clink autorun is fine.

Everything is *installed* correctly (the log shows no failures): `fzf` +
`PSFzf` for pwsh, and `clink` + downloaded `fzf.lua`/`zoxide.lua` for cmd. The
problem is **two independent ordering bugs** that leave the Ctrl+R key binding
un-applied. Symptom is identical in both shells (`bck-i-search:` = the default
readline/PSReadLine incremental search) because in each case the fzf binding is
either clobbered or never persisted, so the shell falls back to its default.

### Bug 1 — pwsh: `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode` clobbers PSFzf's chord

Profile fragments dot-source in numeric order (`10_tools` → `90_psreadline`).

- `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1:59` binds the PSFzf chord
  (`Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'`).
- `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl:19/23` runs **later**
  and calls `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Vi|Windows`, which **resets
  PSReadLine's key-handler tables to that mode's defaults** — reverting Ctrl+r
  to the built-in `ReverseSearchHistory` (`bck-i-search:`) and wiping PSFzf.

This fragment also clobbers *its own* `UpArrow`/`DownArrow` prefix-search
handlers (`90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl:14-15`), because they too are set *before* the
`-EditMode` line. This is the canonical PSFzf gotcha: chords must be applied
**after** `-EditMode`.

### Bug 2 — cmd: `clink set` runs before `fzf.lua` exists

In `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` (the `{{ if .installClink }}` block):

- Line 371 runs `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` **before** the download
  loop at lines 378-382 fetches `fzf.lua` into `%LOCALAPPDATA%\clink`.
- `fzf.default_bindings` is a setting **defined inside `fzf.lua`** (upstream:
  `maybe_add(rl.setbinding and 'fzf.default_bindings', false, …)`, default
  `false`; keys are bound only `if settings.get('fzf.default_bindings')`).
- Per Clink docs, *"scripts are also loaded every time `clink set` is run"* — so
  at line 371 Clink reloads the profile dir, `fzf.lua` isn't there yet, the
  setting is unknown, and the value is **silently not persisted** (`*> $null`
  swallows the error, no `Register-Failure`).
- At cmd runtime `fzf.lua` loads (it downloaded fine), re-registers the setting
  at its default `false`, and never binds Ctrl-R/Ctrl-T/Alt-C.

## Fix

### 1. `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl`

Reorder so `-EditMode` is set **first**, then the history/prediction options,
then the key handlers, then re-apply the PSFzf chord last:

- Move the templated `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Vi|Windows` block to the
  **top** of the `if (Get-Module -Name PSReadLine)` body (right after import).
- Keep the `PredictionSource`/`ViewStyle`/`HistorySearch*` options and the
  `UpArrow`/`DownArrow` handlers after it (they now survive the reset — a bonus
  fix for prefix history search).
- Append, at the **end** of the body:
  \```powershell
  # PSFzf (imported in 10_tools) binds Ctrl+t files / Ctrl+r history. Re-apply
  # here: the -EditMode reset above wipes those bindings.
  if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
      Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
  }
  \```

### 2. `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1`

Remove the now-redundant `Set-PsFzfOption` chord call (lines 58-60); keep the
`Import-Module PSFzf` and the `FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS`/`FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND` env vars.
Leave a one-line comment noting the chord is bound in `90_psreadline` after
`-EditMode`. (Tool import stays in `10_tools`; all PSReadLine key-table
mutations live in `90_psreadline`, in the correct order.)

### 3. `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`

Move `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` (line 371) to **after** the
`foreach ($name in $clinkPlugins.Keys)` download loop (after line 382, still
inside the `if (Have clink)` block), with a comment explaining the ordering:
\```powershell
# fzf.default_bindings is defined INSIDE fzf.lua; `clink set` reloads the
# profile scripts first, so this must run AFTER fzf.lua is downloaded above,
# else the setting is unknown and the value is silently not persisted.
clink set fzf.default_bindings true *> $null    # Ctrl-R history / Ctrl-T files / Alt-C dir
\```
Editing this `run_onchange` changes its content hash, so it re-runs on the next
`chezmoi apply` and actually re-writes the setting on the user's machine.

### 4. Record the pitfall (repo convention)

Per `CLAUDE.md`, add a symptom-titled `pitfalls/ctrl-r-shows-bck-i-search-not-fzf.md`
covering both shells (verbatim symptom `bck-i-search:`, the two root causes, the
ordering fix, and the prevention rule "apply key bindings after `-EditMode` /
after `fzf.lua` is present"). Title by symptom for grep-ability. No `TODO.md`,
docs-table, or CI-flag mirrors are needed — these are bug fixes, not new
prompts/tools.

## Notes / out of scope

- Latent conflict: `10_tools.ps1:46` also runs `atuin init powershell`, which
  would bind Ctrl+R if atuin were installed. It isn't here (else the user would
  see atuin's TUI, not `bck-i-search:`). With this fix PSFzf owns Ctrl+R, matching
  the profile's stated intent. Not changing atuin behavior in this pass.

## Verification

Dev box (macOS — cannot run the Windows scripts; render + parse per CLAUDE.md):

1. Render both templates and syntax-check with the local pwsh:
   \```bash
   TMPD=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$TMPD/home"
   chezmoi init --source="$PWD" --config="$TMPD/c.toml" --persistent-state="$TMPD/s.db" \
     --destination="$TMPD/home" --no-tty <all prompts…>     # include --promptBool 'Install Clink … =true'
   chezmoi execute-template --config="$TMPD/c.toml" --source="$PWD" \
     < dot_config/powershell/profile.d/90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl > /tmp/90.ps1
   chezmoi execute-template --config="$TMPD/c.toml" --source="$PWD" \
     < .chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl > /tmp/pkg.ps1
   pwsh -NoProfile -c "\$null=[ScriptBlock]::Create((Get-Content -Raw /tmp/90.ps1)); \$null=[ScriptBlock]::Create((Get-Content -Raw /tmp/pkg.ps1)); 'parse-ok'"
   \```
2. `pwsh -NoProfile -c "Invoke-ScriptAnalyzer -Path . -Recurse -Settings ./PSScriptAnalyzerSettings.psd1"` (must stay Error-free).
3. Isolated `chezmoi apply … --exclude=scripts` (from CLAUDE.md) to confirm the
   fragments deploy without error.

Windows (real gate — user machine / CI):

- **pwsh**: open a new pwsh, then
  `Get-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Chord 'Ctrl+r'` — Function should be PSFzf's handler,
  **not** `ReverseSearchHistory`. Press Ctrl+R → fzf history pane appears.
- **cmd**: `chezmoi apply` (re-runs the run_onchange), open a **new** cmd, then
  `clink set fzf.default_bindings` should print `true`; `clink info` should list
  `fzf.lua` under loaded scripts; Ctrl+R → fzf history pane.

```

```
</details></tool-use>

     < dot_config/powershell/profile.d/90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl > /tmp/90.ps1
   chezmoi execute-template --config="$TMPD/c.toml" --source="$PWD" \
     < .chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl > /tmp/pkg.ps1
   pwsh -NoProfile -c "\$null=[ScriptBlock]::Create((Get-Content -Raw /tmp/90.ps1)); \$null=[ScriptBlock]::Create((Get-Content -Raw /tmp/pkg.ps1)); 'parse-ok'"
   ```
2. `pwsh -NoProfile -c "Invoke-ScriptAnalyzer -Path . -Recurse -Settings ./PSScriptAnalyzerSettings.psd1"` (must stay Error-free).
3. Isolated `chezmoi apply … --exclude=scripts` (from CLAUDE.md) to confirm the
   fragments deploy without error.

Windows (real gate — user machine / CI):

- **pwsh**: open a new pwsh, then
  `Get-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Chord 'Ctrl+r'` — Function should be PSFzf's handler,
  **not** `ReverseSearchHistory`. Press Ctrl+R → fzf history pane appears.
- **cmd**: `chezmoi apply` (re-runs the run_onchange), open a **new** cmd, then
  `clink set fzf.default_bindings` should print `true`; `clink info` should list
  `fzf.lua` under loaded scripts; Ctrl+R → fzf history pane.
```

README.md
---README---
# Pitfalls

Past traps we've stepped on. **Symptoms-first** knowledge base — when a
problem recurs (on a new machine, after an upgrade, with a new tool combo),
grepping the symptom here lands you on the root cause and workaround in
seconds, instead of re-debugging from scratch.

This folder is excluded from <DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via
.chezmoiignore.tmpl, Python via MANIFEST.in, npm via .npmignore> — it is repo
metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.

## Pitfalls vs the rest

| Surface | Time direction | Question it answers | Access pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| `docs/<tool>.md` | Present | "How does this tool work / how do I configure it?" | Read top to bottom |
| `pitfalls/<slug>.md` | **Past** | **"I see error X — has this happened before?"** | **Grep symptoms** |
| `backlog/<slug>.md` | Future | "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" | Index in `TODO.md` |
| `AGENTS.md` Hard invariants | Present | "What rules MUST agents follow?" | Read top to bottom |

A pitfall **graduates** to a Hard invariant when the trap is serious enough
that you can't rely on memory or grep — typically when (a) it recurs across
machines, (b) it silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
and easy to undo by accident. When graduating, leave a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
as historical record and link to it from the new invariant.

## When to add a pitfall doc

Add `pitfalls/<slug>.md` when you've spent more than ~15 minutes on something
that wasn't googleable, AND any of:

- The symptom is non-obvious from the root cause (silent state, weird side
  effect, behaviour change without error)
- The fix is "do nothing different but in a specific order"
- The same trap could be hit by a new agent / new machine / new contributor
- An upstream bug exists with no ETA — workaround needs to outlive memory
- A specific tool version is required (or forbidden) and failure at the
  wrong version is silent / confusing

## When NOT to add a pitfall doc

- Trivially googleable (next person solves in 30 seconds)
- Already covered in `docs/<tool>.md` — cross-link from this README's
  "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table below instead of duplicating
- Already a Hard invariant (cross-link only)
- One-off transient (network glitch, machine-specific config rot)

## File template

See [`pitfall-doc.md.template`] in the
[`project-knowledge-harness` skill](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/local/project-knowledge-harness)
for the per-doc template.

Key sections (different from `backlog/` template — symptom-first, not
context-first):

```markdown
# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>

**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
**First seen**: YYYY-MM
**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX

## Symptom

Full error messages (verbatim — preserves grep-ability).

## Root cause

Why this happens, with source/docs/upstream issue link.

## Workaround

Copy-pasteable commands or config diff.

## Prevention

How to avoid stepping on this again.
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pitfalls/onedrive-kfm-profile-not-loaded.md`

```text
1	# pwsh $PROFILE not loaded / "is not recognized" under OneDrive Known Folder Move
2	
3	**Symptoms** (grep this section): `is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable`; `Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`; a bare `PS C:\...>` prompt with no starship / no aliases; the error path shows `OneDrive - <tenant>\Documents\PowerShell`.
4	**First seen**: 2026-07
5	**Affects**: Windows with OneDrive "Known Folder Move" (Documents redirected) — common on corporate/managed machines; chezmoi-managed pwsh `$PROFILE`.
6	**Status**: fixed — `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl` now writes the loader **content** (previously a dot-source pointer stub).
7	
8	## Symptom
9	
10	A new pwsh session errors on startup, then falls back to a bare prompt:
11	
12	```
13	. : C:\Users\<user>\OneDrive - <tenant>\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1:4
14	Line |
15	   4 |  . "C:\Users\<user>\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profi …
16	     |    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
17	     | The term 'C:\Users\<user>\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1'
18	     | is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable program.
19	```
20	
21	No starship prompt, no aliases — the managed profile never loaded.
22	
23	## Root cause
24	
25	chezmoi writes the managed loader to the **literal** `~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` (it joins `Documents` off `%USERPROFILE%`, unaware of shell Known Folders). But when OneDrive **Known Folder Move** redirects Documents, interactive pwsh resolves `$PROFILE` to `...\OneDrive - <tenant>\Documents\PowerShell\...` — a *different* path.
26	
27	The old redirect script bridged this by writing a **stub** at the real `$PROFILE` that dot-sourced the literal path:
28	
29	```powershell
30	. "C:\Users\<user>\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1"
31	```
32	
33	Under KFM the literal `~/Documents` copy is **not guaranteed to exist** at `$PROFILE`-load time (OneDrive may have moved/removed it), so the dot-source target is missing → "is not recognized". The stub itself was written fine; only the thing it pointed at was gone. (This is pure path topology — not a privilege issue.)
34	
35	## Workaround
36	
37	Fixed in-repo: the redirect script now writes the managed loader's **content** into `$PROFILE`. The loader is location-independent (it only sources `~/.config/powershell`), so there is nothing left to point at. To recover a broken machine by hand:
38	
39	```powershell
40	chezmoi cat "$HOME\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" |
41	    Set-Content -LiteralPath $PROFILE -Encoding utf8
42	. $PROFILE
43	```
44	
45	`chezmoi cat` renders the managed file from source without needing the literal copy on disk. A following `chezmoi apply` re-runs the redirect script to keep the OneDrive copy in sync.
46	
47	## Prevention
48	
49	- The redirect writes **content, not a pointer**, so a missing literal `~/Documents` copy can no longer break `$PROFILE` load, and there's no self-source recursion risk.
50	- The loader is embedded via `{{ include }}`, so `run_onchange` re-fires whenever the loader changes and the OneDrive copy stays current (the old plain-`.ps1` stub never updated on loader edits).
51	
52	## Related
53	
54	- `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl`
55	- `Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` (the loader)
56	- Graduate to an `AGENTS.md` Hard invariant if it recurs across machines (see `pitfalls/README.md`).
57
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 06:01:26Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# Ctrl+R shows `bck-i-search:` instead of an fzf history pane (pwsh and cmd.exe)

**Symptoms** (grep this section): `bck-i-search:`; Ctrl+R opens the built-in incremental reverse search, not fzf; Ctrl+T / Alt-C do nothing; `fzf`, `PSFzf`, `clink`, `fzf.lua` are all installed and the bootstrap log shows "no failures"; starship still renders in cmd.exe (so clink autorun works) but fzf keys don't; pwsh Up/Down stop doing prefix history search.
**First seen**: 2026-07
**Affects**: this repo's pwsh profile (`PSFzf`) and the opt-in `installClink` cmd.exe integration (`chrisant996/clink-fzf`'s `fzf.lua`). Two independent bugs, one shared symptom.
**Status**: fixed — `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` reordered (EditMode first); `run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` moved `clink set` after the `fzf.lua` download.

## Symptom

In a fresh pwsh **or** cmd.exe session, Ctrl+R shows the default incremental
search prompt instead of fzf:

\```
bck-i-search:
\```

Everything looks installed — `Get-Module -ListAvailable PSFzf` returns a
version, `fzf --version` works, `%LOCALAPPDATA%\clink\fzf.lua` exists, and the
bootstrap log ends with `package install complete — no failures`. In cmd the
starship prompt renders normally, which makes it look like the whole clink
integration is live.

## Root cause

Two separate ordering bugs that both leave the fzf key binding un-applied, so
each shell falls back to its readline/PSReadLine default (`bck-i-search:`).

**pwsh — `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode` resets key handlers.** Profile
fragments dot-source in numeric order (`10_tools` → `90_psreadline`).
`10_tools.ps1` bound the PSFzf chord (`Set-PsFzfOption … -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'`),
then `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` ran `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Windows|Vi`
**afterward**. Setting `-EditMode` rebuilds PSReadLine's key-handler tables from
that mode's defaults, wiping the PSFzf chord (and the `UpArrow`/`DownArrow`
prefix-history handlers, which were also set before the `-EditMode` line in the
same fragment). This is the canonical PSFzf gotcha: chords must be applied
**after** `-EditMode`.

**cmd — `clink set` runs before `fzf.lua` is on disk.** The installer ran
`clink set fzf.default_bindings true` *before* the loop that downloads `fzf.lua`
into `%LOCALAPPDATA%\clink`. `fzf.default_bindings` is a setting **defined
inside `fzf.lua`** (`maybe_add(rl.setbinding and 'fzf.default_bindings', false, …)`;
keys bind only `if settings.get('fzf.default_bindings')`). Per the Clink docs,
*"scripts are also loaded every time `clink set` is run"* — so `clink set`
reloads the profile dir, `fzf.lua` isn't there yet, the setting name is unknown,
and the value is **silently not persisted** (`*> $null` swallowed the error). At
cmd runtime `fzf.lua` loads, re-registers the setting at its default `false`,
and never binds Ctrl-R/Ctrl-T/Alt-C.

## Workaround

Fixed in-repo. To recover a machine by hand without re-applying:

\```powershell
# pwsh — bind after EditMode is already set for the session:
Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
\```

\```cmd
:: cmd — fzf.lua is already downloaded, so this now finds the setting:
clink set fzf.default_bindings true
:: then open a new cmd window (or `clink reload`)
\```

Or just `chezmoi apply` on the fixed repo: the pwsh fragments redeploy, and the
`run_onchange` re-fires (its content hash changed) so `clink set` re-runs *after*
`fzf.lua` exists.

## Prevention

- **Apply key bindings after `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode`.** All PSReadLine
  key-table mutations now live in `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl`, with `-EditMode`
  first and the PSFzf chord last. `10_tools.ps1` only imports PSFzf.
- **Register a script-defined Clink setting before writing it.** `clink set
  <name>` only recognizes settings whose defining `*.lua` is already in the
  profile dir — write the value *after* the download, not before.
- Verify on Windows: pwsh `Get-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Chord 'Ctrl+r'` should not
  be `ReverseSearchHistory`; cmd `clink set fzf.default_bindings` should print
  `true` and `clink info` should list `fzf.lua`.

## Related

- `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` (EditMode + PSFzf chord)
- `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1` (PSFzf import)
- `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` (`installClink` block)
- Upstream: [chrisant996/clink-fzf `fzf.lua`](https://github.com/chrisant996/clink-fzf/blob/master/fzf.lua); [Clink docs](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/clink.html) (scripts reload on `clink set`).

```

```text
=== 90_psreadline rendered (vimMode=true) ===
# 90_psreadline.ps1 — history, inline predictions, and edit mode.
# Templated: edit mode follows the `enableVimMode` init toggle.
# PSReadLine ships with pwsh; import directly rather than scanning PSModulePath
# with Get-Module -ListAvailable. Get-Module -Name checks only loaded modules.
Import-Module PSReadLine -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if (Get-Module -Name PSReadLine) {

    # EditMode MUST come first: -EditMode resets PSReadLine's key-handler tables
    # to that mode's defaults, so every custom handler below (and the PSFzf
    # chords) has to be applied AFTER it or it gets clobbered on each shell start.

    # vi modal editing (mirrors the shells' enableVimMode on macOS/Linux).
    Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Vi
    Set-PSReadLineOption -ViModeIndicator Cursor

    Set-PSReadLineOption -PredictionSource HistoryAndPlugin -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Set-PSReadLineOption -PredictionViewStyle ListView       -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Set-PSReadLineOption -HistorySearchCursorMovesToEnd
    Set-PSReadLineOption -HistoryNoDuplicates

    # Up/Down search history by the prefix already typed.
    Set-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Key UpArrow   -Function HistorySearchBackward
    Set-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Key DownArrow -Function HistorySearchForward

    # PSFzf (imported in 10_tools) binds Ctrl+t files / Ctrl+r history. Re-apply
    # here: the -EditMode reset above wipes those bindings.
    if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
        Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
    }
}
=== 90_psreadline rendered (vimMode=false) ===

    # to that mode's defaults, so every custom handler below (and the PSFzf
    # chords) has to be applied AFTER it or it gets clobbered on each shell start.

    Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode Windows

    Set-PSReadLineOption -PredictionSource HistoryAndPlugin -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Set-PSReadLineOption -PredictionViewStyle ListView       -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Set-PSReadLineOption -HistorySearchCursorMovesToEnd
    Set-PSReadLineOption -HistoryNoDuplicates

    # Up/Down search history by the prefix already typed.
    Set-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Key UpArrow   -Function HistorySearchBackward
    Set-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Key DownArrow -Function HistorySearchForward

    # PSFzf (imported in 10_tools) binds Ctrl+t files / Ctrl+r history. Re-apply
    # here: the -EditMode reset above wipes those bindings.
    if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
        Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
    }
}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-11 06:03:24Z)**_

All done. Here's what I fixed and verified.

## Root cause: two separate ordering bugs, one symptom

Both fzf installs succeeded — only the key *bindings* silently failed:

- **pwsh:** `10_tools.ps1` bound the PSFzf chord, then `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` ran `Set-PSReadLineOption -EditMode` *afterward*, which resets PSReadLine's key-handler tables to defaults → Ctrl+R reverted to the built-in `bck-i-search:`. (This also silently broke Up/Down prefix-history search, since those handlers were set before `-EditMode` too.)
- **cmd:** `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` ran *before* `fzf.lua` was downloaded. Since that setting is defined *inside* `fzf.lua` and `clink set` reloads the profile scripts first, the setting was unknown and never persisted → `fzf.lua` loaded at its default `false` and bound nothing. (Starship worked because clink autorun + `starship.lua` are independent of that toggle — which is why cmd *looked* fully wired.)

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` | `-EditMode` now set **first**; Up/Down handlers + PSFzf chord re-applied **after** it |
| `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1` | Dropped the now-clobbered `Set-PsFzfOption` (kept `Import-Module PSFzf` + env vars); comment points to `90_psreadline` |
| `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` | Moved `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` to **after** the `fzf.lua` download loop |
| `pitfalls/ctrl-r-shows-bck-i-search-not-fzf.md` | New symptom-titled pitfall doc (per repo convention) |

## Verified (dev-box render + parse)

- Rendered `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl` in **both** vim and Windows edit-mode branches — `-EditMode` is first in each.
- Rendered the packages script with `installClink=true` — `clink set` now sits after the download loop.
- Parsed all rendered files + `10_tools.ps1` with local pwsh → `parse-ok`.
- PSScriptAnalyzer → **no Errors** (the 3 warnings are pre-existing empty-catch blocks in `bootstrap.ps1`).

## To apply on your Windows box

```
chezmoi apply
```

The `run_onchange` re-fires (its hash changed), so `clink set` re-runs *after* `fzf.lua` exists. Then open a **new** pwsh **and** a new cmd window:

- **pwsh:** `Get-PSReadLineKeyHandler -Chord 'Ctrl+r'` should no longer be `ReverseSearchHistory`; Ctrl+R → fzf pane.
- **cmd:** `clink set fzf.default_bindings` should print `true`; Ctrl+R → fzf pane.

One note I left out of scope: `10_tools.ps1` also runs `atuin init powershell`, which would also claim Ctrl+R *if* atuin were installed. It isn't on your box (you'd see atuin's TUI, not `bck-i-search:`), and with this fix PSFzf owns Ctrl+R per the profile's stated intent. Say the word if you'd rather atuin win Ctrl+R when present.

The changes aren't committed — let me know if you'd like me to commit them (with the SpecStory transcript, per your repo's history-hygiene convention).

_**User (2026-07-11 06:04:21Z)**_

Base directory for this skill: /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles/.claude/skills/agent-history-hygiene

# agent-history-hygiene

Keep agent chat transcripts and plan files committed together with the
code they produced, without leaking secrets. Pairs with the
`redact-agent-secrets` + `gitleaks` pre-commit hooks the skill installs.

Three surfaces, separated by purpose:

| Surface                          | Question it answers                                          |
|----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `find-session.sh`                | "Which transcript / plan file is *my* current session?"      |
| `stage-agent-artifacts.sh`       | "Which agent files belong in the next commit?"               |
| `bootstrap-project.sh`           | "How do I get pre-commit + gitleaks + redactor into a repo?" |
| `scan-staged.sh`                 | "Is there a leaked secret in what I'm about to commit?"      |
| `references/remediation.md`      | "I already pushed a secret — now what?"                      |

## Core invariants

1. **Agent transcripts and plan files are committed alongside the diff
   that produced them.** Never add them to `.gitignore`. An agent that
   drops these from a commit has broken the user's review trail.
2. **Rotate at the provider before any git rewrite.** The only act
   that revokes a leaked credential is rotation. History rewriting
   scrubs bytes on one clone and leaves them on every other.
3. **`git push --force` against a shared branch is never the fix for a
   leak.** At best it's useless; at worst it destroys teammate work and
   silently re-introduces the secret when someone merges their old
   history back.

## When to use this skill

Use it when the user (or you) surface any of:

- "Commit my chat" / "save the specstory session" / "include the plan
  file in this commit" / "把 plan 跟 specstory 一起 commit 進去".
- You see dirty `.specstory/history/*.md`, `.claude/plans/*.md`,
  `.cursor/plans/*.md`, or any other configured agent artifact during
  `git status` and you're about to commit a feature.
- "Scrub this transcript" / "redact my key" / "gitleaks flagged my
  chat history".
- "Set up pre-commit for this repo" / "I'm starting a new project — how
  do I get the hook stack?" / "bootstrap secret scanning here".
- "I pushed a `.env`" / "a secret went to main" / "do I need to force
  push?" — the agent must steer to `references/remediation.md` and
  stop the user from force-pushing reflexively.

## When NOT to use

- The user explicitly wants agent transcripts **excluded** from the
  repo. Respect that; suggest a one-liner `.gitignore` addition and
  skip this skill entirely.
- The leak is already on a shared `main`/release branch. Do **not**
  offer to rewrite history — jump to `references/remediation.md` §5.
- The project genuinely has no agent session (no `.specstory/`, no
  `.claude/plans/`, etc.). Nothing to stage.
- Single-file, single-commit hygiene that the agent handles without any
  script (e.g., adding a missing trailing newline).

## Integration with existing infrastructure

This skill sits **on top of** any chezmoi-managed stack the user
already has. It does not duplicate:

- **chezmoi's global `core.hooksPath`** (`~/.config/git/hooks/pre-commit`)
  — that wrapper runs the repo's `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and then
  optionally `gitleaks git --staged`. The skill bootstraps the repo-level
  config the wrapper expects to find.
- **chezmoi's `.gitleaks.toml`** — the user's config already carries
  curated rules for common API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, Supabase,
  Linear, WakaTime, Cursor, HuggingFace, Notion, Tailscale, Clash /
  V2Ray tokens). The skill's `assets/gitleaks.toml.template` ships the
  same rule IDs so `.gitleaksignore` / allowlist tweaks stay portable.
- **chezmoi's `scripts/redact_secrets.py`** — remains the upstream
  source of truth. The skill bundles a copy so non-chezmoi users get
  protection; sync procedure in
  [`references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md`](references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md).

What this skill **adds**:

- Agent-facing discipline (this `SKILL.md` + `references/remediation.md`).
- A single-command project bootstrap (`bootstrap-project.sh`) for repos
  without chezmoi or where the user wants the stack in one go.
- Session-discovery heuristics (`find-session.sh`) for the "find my
  current transcript among many" problem.
- An exit-code wrapper (`scan-staged.sh`) agents can branch on before
  committing.

## Workflow A: commit-time hygiene

Default flow when the agent is about to commit feature changes plus
chat/plan artifacts.

```bash
# 1. Make sure the agent knows which session is "ours" — mostly
#    relevant when multiple Claude/SpecStory sessions run in the repo.
bash skills/local/agent-history-hygiene/scripts/find-session.sh

# 2. Stage code the usual way, then auto-add agent artifacts.
git add path/to/feature/file.ts
bash skills/local/agent-history-hygiene/scripts/stage-agent-artifacts.sh
# Use --session-only if you want ONLY the current SpecStory + newest plan;
# default stages every dirty *.md in every configured agent dir.

# 3. Belt-and-suspenders secret scan before commit. Exit 0 = clean.
bash skills/local/agent-history-hygiene/scripts/scan-staged.sh || {
  # Exit 10/20: leaks found. Jump to references/remediation.md.
  echo "Leaks detected — see references/remediation.md before committing." >&2
  exit 1
}

# 4. Commit. pre-commit (installed by bootstrap) runs redact-agent-secrets
#    then gitleaks again as a catch-all.
git commit -m "feat: ..."
```

## Workflow B: bootstrap a new project

For repos that don't yet have `.pre-commit-config.yaml` / `.gitleaks.toml`
installed. Runs once per repo.

```bash
cd /path/to/new/project
bash skills/local/agent-history-hygiene/scripts/bootstrap-project.sh \
  --install-hook            # optional: auto-stage on every commit

# Verify: shake out any existing issues in the working tree.
pre-commit run --all-files
```

What `bootstrap-project.sh` does:

1. Drops `.pre-commit-config.yaml` + `.gitleaks.toml` into the repo
   (skips if already present unless `--force`).
2. Writes `scripts/redact_secrets.py` (bundled copy; use
   `--from-chezmoi` to symlink the chezmoi source so fixes propagate).
3. Runs `pre-commit install` (or `uvx pre-commit@4 install` if
   pre-commit isn't on `PATH`).
4. Audits `.gitignore` / `.git/info/exclude` for patterns that would
   silently hide an agent artifact dir — warns without editing.
5. Checks `~/.claude/settings.json` for `plansDirectory`; prints the
   one-line patch if missing.
6. With `--install-hook`: writes a `prepare-commit-msg` hook that calls
   `stage-agent-artifacts.sh --session-only --allow-empty` so every
   `git commit` auto-attaches the current session file.

## Workflow C: post-leak remediation

When `scan-staged.sh` reports exit `10`/`20`, or the user says "I
committed / pushed a secret":

1. **STOP** the user from running `git push --force` reflexively.
2. Read `references/remediation.md` end-to-end.
3. Walk the user through **step 1 (rotate)** regardless of blast
   radius. Only after rotation does the question of scrubbing history
   become worth discussing.
4. Use the decision tree in the runbook to pick the right git action.

## Gotchas

- **`plansDirectory` project-level sometimes ignored.** Claude Code
  issue [#19537](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/19537)
  reports project-level `plansDirectory` being ignored in some
  versions. After running a `/plan`, verify the file actually landed
  where you expected before relying on `stage-agent-artifacts.sh`
  picking it up. User-level config (`~/.claude/settings.json` with
  `"plansDirectory": "./.claude/plans"`) is the recommended default.
- **`gitleaks protect` is deprecated.** Since v8.19.0 use
  `gitleaks git --staged --redact` (pre-commit) and
  `gitleaks dir <path>` (working directory). The older commands still
  work but emit a deprecation notice. This skill uses the modern
  syntax everywhere.
- **`pre-commit install` is per-clone.** Each teammate must run
  `pre-commit install` in their own clone for hooks to fire. CI cannot
  be trusted as the single gate — it's second-chance, not last-chance.
- **Transcript files can be huge.** A long SpecStory session can exceed
  2 MB. The template bumps `check-added-large-files` to `--maxkb=2048`
  to avoid false positives, but a very long session can still overflow.
  If you hit the limit, rotate sessions (`specstory run claude`
  creates a fresh file) instead of raising the cap further.
- **Session-UUID divergence between SpecStory CLI and VS Code
  extension.** The extension autosaves into `.specstory/history/`
  continuously; the CLI (`specstory run claude`) creates one file per
  invocation. If both are active you can end up with two transcripts
  for what feels like "one session" — mtime-newest wins in
  `find-session.sh`.
- **Global `core.hooksPath` means bare repos aren't protected.** The
  chezmoi setup's global hook runs `.pre-commit-config.yaml` IF it
  exists — so a repo without `.pre-commit-config.yaml` has no
  protection. Run `bootstrap-project.sh` before the first commit with
  agent artifacts, not after.
- **Active SpecStory writer can defeat the redact loop.** The standard
  `git add → git commit → pre-commit auto-fixes → re-stage → re-commit`
  flow assumes the file is **quiescent** during the commit. SpecStory's
  `specstory_*_watch` daemon tails the agent transcript continuously,
  so if the chat captured `ps -axo args`-style output that contained an
  unrelated daemon's secret in argv (e.g. SpecStory's own
  `--cloud-token …` flag), every diagnostic command (`grep`, `sed -n
  '<line>p'`, `cat | head | tail`) prints the secret again, SpecStory
  appends it to the transcript, and the redact-then-restage cycle
  never converges. Symptom: pre-commit says "Successfully redacted N
  file(s)" but `gitleaks-system` immediately fails on the same line,
  re-running `git add && git commit` doesn't help, and `grep -c
  '<secret-prefix>' file` shows the count *increasing* over commit
  attempts. **Workaround**: a single atomic
  `python3 -c "<in-place re.sub>" && git add <file> && git commit -m
  "..."` pipeline so the index is frozen before any new specstory write
  lands. **Don't** print, grep, or diff the secret line during the
  recovery — every print echoes back into the transcript. Diagnose with
  `lsof <file>` (looking for `specstory_*` writers) instead. See
  `pitfalls/redact-secrets-loop-with-active-specstory-writer.md` in
  upstream chezmoi for the full debugging trail.

## Available scripts

- **`scripts/find-session.sh [--format=specstory|claude|both] [--json]`**
  Discover the current agent session files for `$PWD`. TSV default,
  `--json` for structured callers. Never exits non-zero (always 0,
  empty fields signal absence).

- **`scripts/stage-agent-artifacts.sh [--session-only] [--include-all-plans] [--dry-run] [--allow-empty]`**
  `git add` the right agent artifacts before the next commit.
  `--session-only` stages only the current SpecStory + newest plan;
  default stages every dirty `*.md` in every configured artifact dir.
  Refuses to run if there are no code changes (prevents "commit just
  transcript"); override with `--allow-empty`.

- **`scripts/scan-staged.sh [--redact] [--verbose]`**
  Run `gitleaks git --staged` with agent-friendly exit codes
  (0 clean / 10 redacted / 20 leaks / 30 gitleaks missing). JSON lines
  on stdout, prose diagnostics on stderr.

- **`scripts/bootstrap-project.sh [--from-chezmoi] [--install-hook] [--force] [--dry-run]`**
  Install `.pre-commit-config.yaml` + `.gitleaks.toml` + the redactor
  into the current repo, then run `pre-commit install`. Audits
  `.gitignore` and `~/.claude/settings.json` for misconfigurations
  (warns, never silently edits).

## Bundled assets

- `assets/artifact-dirs.txt` — the canonical list of agent artifact
  directories (SpecStory, Claude plans, Cursor plans + rules, OpenCode
  plans, Spec-kit, Codex). Consumed by `stage-agent-artifacts.sh` and
  by `bootstrap-project.sh` when rendering the pre-commit `files:`
  regex.
- `assets/pre-commit-config.yaml.template` — minimal
  `.pre-commit-config.yaml` with `redact-agent-secrets` + gitleaks +
  standard hygiene hooks.
- `assets/gitleaks.toml.template` — portable subset of the chezmoi
  `.gitleaks.toml` with custom rule IDs + a path-scoped allowlist for
  agent artifact dirs.
- `assets/redact_secrets.py` — bundled copy of chezmoi's
  `scripts/redact_secrets.py`. Chezmoi version is upstream; sync doc
  in `references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md`.

## Reference files

- [`references/transcript-session-discovery.md`](references/transcript-session-discovery.md)
  — SpecStory / Claude session layouts and the `$PWD → slug` algorithm.
  Read when `find-session.sh` returns empty or ambiguous results.
- [`references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md`](references/pre-commit-redaction-stack.md)
  — three-layer defense (redact → gitleaks → `scan-staged.sh`),
  allowlist design, sync procedure for the bundled redactor. Read
  when tuning rules or debugging unexpected pre-commit failures.
- [`references/remediation.md`](references/remediation.md) —
  rotate-first runbook for "I committed / pushed a secret". Read
  **before** any `git filter-repo` / `git push --force` action.

## Tests

The skill ships with a three-level test suite under
[`tests/`](tests/README.md). Run from repo root:

```bash
make test-skill
```

- `test_redact_secrets.py` — pytest for pure redactor functions.
- `test_gitleaks_corpus.py` — golden-corpus fixtures staged in tmp git
  repos, asserting real-key shapes fire and example shapes are
  allowlisted only inside configured artifact dirs.
- `test_scan_staged.sh` — exit-code contract for
  `scripts/scan-staged.sh` (0 / 20 / 30 / 2).

The corpus + shell tests skip gracefully when `gitleaks` isn't on
`PATH`. See [`tests/README.md`](tests/README.md) for what each
regression the suite locks in.

## Related skills

- [`project-knowledge-harness`](../project-knowledge-harness/SKILL.md)
  — complementary memory harness (TODO.md + backlog/ + pitfalls/) that
  references `.claude/plans/` as "ephemeral agent scratchpads". This
  skill fills the gap: those scratchpads belong in git, not ignored.

fix(fzf): bind Ctrl+R in pwsh and cmd — chords must apply after EditMode / after fzf.lua

Both fzf integrations installed cleanly but silently failed to bind Ctrl+R,
falling back to the built-in bck-i-search reverse search. Two independent
ordering bugs, one symptom.

pwsh: PSFzf set its Ctrl+t/Ctrl+r chords in 10_tools, then 90_psreadline ran

key-handler tables to that mode's defaults and wiped the chord. Reorder

history search, which was clobbered the same way.

cmd: `clink set fzf.default_bindings true` ran before the loop that downloads
fzf.lua. That setting is defined inside fzf.lua and `clink set` reloads the
profile scripts first, so the name was unknown and the value silently not
persisted. Move it after the plugin download loop.

Add pitfalls/ctrl-r-shows-bck-i-search-not-fzf.md documenting both.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "=== commit exit: $? ==="
git log --oneline -1
